adversarial_risks.risk: A prompts can leak decisive evidence through case titles, source-boundary notes, or summaries even when explicit evidence fields are omitted.
adversarial_risks.current_mitigation: Phase 3 repaired A prompts with sanitized visible labels, summaries, and source-boundary phrases; final validation errors are zero.
adversarial_risks.remaining_risk: Manual future edits could reintroduce leakage.
adversarial_risks.future_check: Rerun prompt-contract and v2 leakage checks after any prompt edit.

adversarial_risks.risk: B compact evidence may remain sufficient for full score, reproducing the repaired benchmark ceiling.
adversarial_risks.current_mitigation: Cases include alias reconciliation, domain obligations, first-gap localization, matched/missing ledgers, and non-claim boundaries.
adversarial_risks.remaining_risk: Strong downstream agents may still infer the intended artifact from compact evidence.
adversarial_risks.future_check: Score per-case C-sensitivity dimensions as explanatory and avoid C-over-B promotion unless predeclared required dimensions separate.

adversarial_risks.risk: C framing can become a disguised answer key if it names evaluator outcomes or first-failure labels.
adversarial_risks.current_mitigation: C prompts include decision criteria and requested artifact structures, not manifest-only expected output fields or evaluator labels.
adversarial_risks.remaining_risk: Some requested artifact wording may still make expected output style easier.
adversarial_risks.future_check: Treat C advantage as valid only when task/evidence quality improves without hidden labels or authority statements.

adversarial_risks.risk: Scoring may reward polished packet format instead of mathematical task success.
adversarial_risks.current_mitigation: Scoring applicability map keeps frozen required dimensions primary and labels candidate-only stressors as explanatory unless approved before collection.
adversarial_risks.remaining_risk: Human scorer may overvalue completeness of sections.
adversarial_risks.future_check: Score hard-veto-first and require task outcome, evidence use, assumptions/gaps, boundaries, and actionability before crediting format.

adversarial_risks.risk: Synthetic fixtures may be easier, cleaner, or less representative than real local source tasks.
adversarial_risks.current_mitigation: Synthetic cases are modeled on repaired benchmark failure modes and workflow families, while avoiding private-source excerpts.
adversarial_risks.remaining_risk: Future result remains a local diagnostic, not public/scientific/model reliability evidence.
adversarial_risks.future_check: Keep non-claims in result notes; add real bounded cases only under a separate source-boundary review.

adversarial_risks.risk: One response per prompt can be noisy and cannot establish general model reliability.
adversarial_risks.current_mitigation: Future runbook requires explicit approval for response subject, prompt count, retry policy, and artifact paths; replication is a separate approval choice.
adversarial_risks.remaining_risk: Single-run diagnostic may still be overinterpreted.
adversarial_risks.future_check: State one-response results as local diagnostic only; require preapproved replication for stronger claims.

ceiling_effect_countermeasures.countermeasure: Compact scalar evidence is insufficient without matrix/domain route obligations.
ceiling_effect_countermeasures.residual_ceiling_risk: B may still abstain correctly if it notices the domain marker.

ceiling_effect_countermeasures.countermeasure: Witness evidence must be scoped through a domain map.
ceiling_effect_countermeasures.residual_ceiling_risk: B may correctly preserve the unrestricted/restricted distinction from terse notes.

ceiling_effect_countermeasures.countermeasure: Same-scalar and mask/domain assumptions must be organized into a ledger.
ceiling_effect_countermeasures.residual_ceiling_risk: B may list all assumptions from compact route notes.

ceiling_effect_countermeasures.countermeasure: A matched/missing-term ledger is required and local/global code claims must be separated.
ceiling_effect_countermeasures.residual_ceiling_risk: B may infer the missing solve contribution from compact AST evidence.
The trace may be simple enough for B to localize correctly.

ceiling_effect_countermeasures.countermeasure: The expected artifact is a diagnostic packet, not a proof.
ceiling_effect_countermeasures.residual_ceiling_risk: B may produce a good packet from the compact facts alone.

decision_table.decision: v2 candidate prompts are ready for future approved collection, not collection now
decision_table.primary_criterion_status: prompt validation and adversarial analysis pass
decision_table.veto_diagnostic_status: no response collection, no Claude worker role, no prompt-contract errors, no repaired baseline mutation
decision_table.main_uncertainty: whether B remains at ceiling under future response collection
decision_table.next_justified_action: write final candidate handoff with explicit collection approval requirements
decision_table.what_is_not_concluded: no C-over-B superiority, model reliability, public benchmark validity, release readiness, scientific validation, or product capability

non_claims: This analysis is not scored response evidence.
This analysis does not prove v2 will separate B and C.
This analysis does not authorize response collection.
This analysis is not release, public benchmark, scientific, product, or general model reliability evidence.
